name: Concrete CMS (concrete5) REST API
what_it_does: Open-source PHP CMS with REST API — enabling government agencies, universities, and enterprises to manage web content with inline editing, a visual page builder, user management, workflow approvals, permissions, multilingual support, and headless delivery via REST API, with strong government sector adoption in the US.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via Concrete CMS REST API for decoupled frontend applications
Managing Concrete CMS pages and blocks via API for content migration automation
Querying Concrete CMS users and permissions via API for organizational directory integration
Managing Concrete CMS file manager assets via API for digital asset automation
Integrating Concrete CMS with external systems via API for enterprise content workflows
not_for: Teams without PHP expertise (Concrete CMS is PHP-based — requires developer)
High-speed startup teams needing modern DX (Concrete CMS targets government/enterprise sector)
Teams needing modern API-first headless architecture (Concrete CMS adds REST as addon to traditional CMS)
best_when: A US government agency, university, or enterprise needs a production-grade PHP CMS with strong WCAG accessibility, complex page workflows, fine-grained permissions, and government sector experience.
avoid_when: You don't have PHP expertise, need modern API-first headless CMS, or want a lighter-weight developer CMS.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow for API access. Client ID + Secret from Concrete CMS admin. Bearer token auth. Scoped access (read, write per content type). No webhooks. REST API built-in to Concrete CMS 9+. Token management via Concrete CMS admin panel. PHP session auth for admin panel. Role-based permissions system.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source (MIT license) — completely free self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: Concrete CMS Cloud from $19/month (managed); Enterprise support plans
pricing.notes: Open source project. Created by Franz Maruna in 2003. MIT license. Previously named concrete5, rebranded to Concrete CMS (2021). GitHub: concretecms/concretecms (4,000+ stars). PHP 8+ + MySQL/PostgreSQL. Strong US government adoption — Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance. Used by: state and local governments, universities, healthcare, non-profits. Inline editing (edit-in-place UX). Express (database-backed content). Workflow approvals. Multilingual with Multilingual package. Concrete CMS Cloud for managed hosting. REST API added in v9 (2021+). Marketplace for add-ons.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API in v9+. OAuth2 client credentials with scopes. No webhooks. PHP expertise required. Self-hosted. No rate limits. Section 508/WCAG accessibility focus. US government sector use cases. REST API added relatively recently (2021) — less mature than other PHP CMSs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Page reads idempotent by ID or path. File reads idempotent. User reads idempotent. Create operations not idempotent — check by path. OAuth2 tokens refresh automatically.
known_agent_gotchas: REST API added in v9 — older installations (v8) don't have REST API
No webhooks — must poll for content changes
PHP expertise required for customization beyond REST API
Government sector user — WCAG and Section 508 compliance expectations
Self-hosted only in base — Concrete CMS Cloud for managed option

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config. User-controlled data residency. OAuth2 with scoped access. Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for government use. Role-based permissions. Open source — auditable. PHP security model. No webhooks reduces outbound data exposure. User responsible for server hardening. Government sector experience implies strong security focus.
